steps.title: Use one poem as evidence
steps.content: The student wants to use one poem to illustrate the sestina form. The sentence must identify a single poem, explain the form's thirty-nine lines and six patterned end words, and use end words that belong to that poem.

steps.title: Reject inaccurate examples
steps.content: Choice A names both poems instead of using one as the illustration. Choice C assigns the end words from Towards Autumn to Forage Sestina. Choice D assigns the end words from Forage Sestina to Towards Autumn. The answer is B.
